edges.description: Blocked ceramide catabolism causes lysosomal ceramide accumulation.
Ceramide storage is associated with granulomas containing lipid-filled macrophages.
Ceramide storage and storage-associated inflammation are reflected in the patient plasma cytokine and ceramide profile.
intermediate_mechanisms:
Storage-associated macrophage infiltration and abnormal hematopoietic activation elevate inflammatory mediators.
Disease-associated ceramide species accumulate systemically and are detectable in plasma.

edges.description: Lysosomal ceramide storage is reflected by increased disease-associated ceramide species.
Ceramide storage can involve CNS cell types and contribute to neurologic disease.
Multisystem ceramide storage can involve the respiratory system.
Severe multisystem storage disease can manifest as failure to thrive.
Tissue ceramide storage extends to the retina, where acid ceramidase restoration reduces ceramide accumulation and macrophage activation in a Farber disease mouse model.
edges.target: Retinal ceramide storage and macrophage activation

edges.description: Granulomatous macrophage infiltration produces subcutaneous and periarticular nodules.
Cartilage and periarticular inflammation contributes to arthritis.
Cartilage and periarticular inflammation contributes to painful joints.
Periarticular storage and inflammation contribute to joint contractures.
Periarticular inflammation contributes to swollen joints.
Macrophage-filled laryngeal nodules contribute to progressive hoarseness.
Inflammatory mediator activation contributes to rheumatologic presentation and JIA-like misdiagnosis.
Inflammatory mediator activation contributes to swollen inflammatory joints.
CNS involvement contributes to developmental delay in affected children.
CNS involvement contributes to cognitive impairment.
CNS storage includes foam-cell and microglial/macrophage pathology.
Early neurologic involvement contributes to infantile hypotonia.
Retinal storage disease is consistent with macular cherry-red spot annotation.
edges.source: Retinal ceramide storage and macrophage activation
edges.target: Cherry red spot of the macula

nodes.description: AAV-mediated ASAH1 over-expression is a preclinical ocular gene-therapy strategy that reduced retinal ceramide accumulation and macrophage activation in a Farber mouse model; it remains experimental.

evidence.reference_title: rAAV-mediated over-expression of acid ceramidase prevents retinopathy in a mouse model of Farber lipogranulomatosis.
evidence.snippet: This study represents the first evidence of a gene therapy for Farber disease-related retinopathy.

nodes.description: Biallelic ASAH1 pathogenic variants reduce or abolish lysosomal acid ceramidase activity, the N-acylsphingosine amidohydrolase that normally hydrolyzes ceramide to sphingosine and fatty acid.
